mnemonics: A Very Cool Identity Element (A.V.C.I.E.) - A group must have A: Associativity, V: an inverse for every element, C: Closure, I: an Identity element.
keyPoints: A group is a set G with a binary operation that satisfies four axioms: closure, associativity, identity element, and inverse element.
The identity element e in a group is unique and satisfies a*e = e*a = a for all a in G.
Every element a in a group has a unique inverse a⁻¹ such that a*a⁻¹ = a⁻¹*a = e.
commonMistakes: Assuming commutativity: a group does not necessarily have to be commutative (Abelian); a*b = b*a is not a group axiom.
Forgetting to check closure: the result of the operation on any two elements must always be back in the set G.
Confusing the identity element: assuming 0 is always the identity; in multiplicative groups, the identity is often 1.
